title: v0.0.1 visual-fidelity gap: global CSS layer never implemented (starter App.css, no reset, undefined --tux-* tokens, light form controls)
description: Found at M2 smoke 2026-05-20. The main-UI build gated logic + functional structure but had NO visual-fidelity gate, so the GLOBAL design layer was never implemented and the shipped UI diverges sharply from docs/design/v0.0.1-ux-mockups.md + docs/design/mockups/images/. Specific VERIFIED findings (worktree bd-tuxlink-8zg-integration-wiring @ feat 3a94790): (1) NO global reset — default body{margin:8px} offsets the 100vw/100vh .app-shell from top/left + overflows viewport ('floating' bug); add *,*::before,*::after{box-sizing:border-box} + html,body,#root{margin:0;height:100%}. (2) src/App.css is the unmodified create-tauri-app starter — light :root (#0f0f0f on #f6f6f6), .logo/.container/.row dead rules, light form-control theming -> inputs/buttons render LIGHT on the dark shell. Replace with the real design system. (3) The --tux-* design tokens (--tux-bg/fg/border/hover/accent-bg/badge) are used as fallbacks in component CSS but NEVER defined centrally -> no theme source of truth; define them on :root from the mock palette. (4) No box-sizing:border-box -> paddings inflate widths past grid tracks -> cramming/overlap. (5) index.html <title> still 'Tauri + React + Typescript'. NOTE: per-component CSS (AppShell grid, DashboardRibbon, StatusBar, MessageView, Compose) is largely sound + matches the mock LAYOUT; the gap is the global foundation + mock-fidelity polish (typography/spacing/aesthetic), not a rebuild. Suggest two tiers: (A) global-CSS foundation fix (reset/box-sizing/tokens/base+form theming/retitle) — fixes the reported floating+cramming+theme-clash; (B) a frontend-design fidelity pass against the mocks. PROCESS follow-up: add a visual-smoke / design-fidelity gate to the build pipeline so this can't recur.
notes: FOUNDATION SLICE LANDED via PR #85 (commit 0c01f75): global reset + box-sizing (fixes floating + cramming), central --tux-* tokens + amber accent from mock-d, dark base + form-control theming (fixes light wizard/forms), retitle. Operator smoke-verified 'much cleaner'. REMAINING (this issue): fidelity-polish pass — reconcile Compose.css (self-contained Catppuccin palette, ignores --tux-*) onto the central tokens; apply amber accent to components (Reply button, unread dots); match the mocks (typography/spacing/aesthetic) per docs/design/mockups/images/mock-d-mailapp-minimal.png.

title: Keyring integration tests write to the operator's REAL system keyring (XDG_DATA_HOME/HOME not isolated)
description: ROOT CAUSE of the 2026-05-20 keyring breakage. tuxlink's keyring integration tests call entry.set_password() against the real Secret Service: wizard_integration_test.rs:92/209 ('should write to real keyring') and wizard_persist_cms_test.rs:171. The XdgGuard (wizard_integration_test.rs:47-53) isolates ONLY XDG_CONFIG_HOME. gnome-keyring stores under XDG_DATA_HOME / HOME (~/.local/share/keyrings/), which is NOT isolated; dbus-run-session isolates only the D-Bus session bus, not the on-disk keyring files. So 'isolated' keyring tests AND any setup recipe (e.g. SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/run/user/1000/keyring/ssh with a typed password) reach straight into the operator's REAL, cross-project-shared (geographica also uses secret-tool against this same login keyring) system keyring. On 2026-05-20 a manual setup one-liner (bash history line 1809) re-keyed the operator's login keyring with an unrecoverable password, breaking secret access for BOTH tuxlink and geographica until a reset. FIX: (1) keyring tests MUST set a throwaway HOME (or XDG_DATA_HOME) so gnome-keyring uses a temp keyring dir, IN ADDITION to dbus-run-session; assert the test never resolves to ~/.local/share/keyrings. (2) Provide a documented safe headless-keyring-test recipe (ephemeral HOME + dbus-run-session + gnome-keyring-daemon --unlock with EMPTY password inside the sandbox). (3) Purge any setup guidance that runs --unlock against the real keyring. Cross-project hazard; bites the shared-CI/dev-environment transfer goal.

title: Define WinlinkBackend trait — v0.5 prep Step 1 (Pat greenfield refactor)
description:
Operator decision 2026-05-18 (Codex + Cameron convergence): tuxlink moves off Pat to native Rust at v0.5 via staged replacement (not flag-day). Step 1 is the architectural boundary — define a WinlinkBackend trait that decouples tuxlink's UI/config layer from the Pat HTTP sidecar so future native + Pat implementations can co-exist behind one interface.

Trait surface (from current Pat usage in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs + pat-client integration):
- list_messages(folder: Folder) -> Vec<MessageMeta>
- read_message(id: MessageId) -> Message
- send_message(msg: OutboundMessage) -> Result<MessageId>
- connect(transport: TransportConfig) -> Result<SessionHandle>
- disconnect(session: SessionHandle) -> Result<()>
- status() -> BackendStatus
- stream_log() -> impl Stream<Item = LogLine>

Two conceptual impls:
1. PatBackend — wraps current HTTP-to-Pat-sidecar. Lands in this issue's PR.
2. NativeBackend — placeholder/stub in this issue; fleshed out in Steps 3-10.

Discipline: this IS a hard-to-undo architectural decision (the trait shape constrains every future backend implementation), so build-robust-features applies — BUT TIGHTLY SCOPED per the operator's 2026-05-18 framing:
- Brainstorm: 1 hour max (concept only — error model, async vs sync, session-handle ownership, log-stream cancellation)
- Spec: trait definition + behavior contract + 5-10 test cases (NOT 24+)
- >=1 cross-provider adrev round (NOT 5 — Codex review on the trait surface only)
- Plan: trivial (trait file + stub NativeBackend + PatBackend wrapper around current pat-client code)
- Ship as one PR

Follow-up steps (each gets its own bd issue when this one merges):
2. Freeze Pat backend as reference (PatBackend implements the trait)
3. Build native mailbox + B2F parser/writer with golden fixtures from Pat / wl2k-go
4. Build B2F session state machine over generic async IO
5. Build CMS telnet client + fake CMS harness
6. Pat-vs-native parity tests over same fixtures
7. Operator-only live CMS smoke gate
8. AX.25/KISS layer (KISS = protocol compliance per memory feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability)
9. Native VARA as separate milestone (clean-room: reverse-engineering writeups read as inverse spec, NOT adoption guide)
10. Default native after parity+soak; remove Pat sidecar after one fallback release

Memories to read before starting:
- feedback_discipline_triage_rule (this IS architectural; pipeline applies, but tightly scoped)
- feedback_clean_sheet_concepts_only
- feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability
- project_v05_modem_design_posture
- project_g90_vara_standard_works_firsthand
- feedback_no_atomic_decisions_to_operator

Replaces nothing in v0.0.1 — this is purely v0.5 prep. v0.0.1 continues to ship the Pat sidecar via the cred-handling fork (PR-A merged + PR-B merged); the trait wraps that existing surface without disturbing the shipped wizard or PatProcess flow.

title: Task 3 (PatProcess) amendment — render Pat's non-secret config at spawn time
description:
HARD prerequisite for wizard-cluster impl (tuxlink-ln3 / Tasks 9/10/11/11.5). Surfaced by Codex R5 P1 on wizard-cluster spec adrev §7.3 item 4.

Pre-cred-handling: Pat's config.json was pre-existing or operator-managed; PatProcess just spawned the binary.
Post-cred-handling (PR-A merged): Pat's config.json no longer carries the WL2K password (deleted from cfg.SecureLoginPassword); Pat reads password from OS keyring. BUT Pat still expects its own config.json with non-secret fields (callsign, MBO address, transport selection).

Wizard now writes: (1) tuxlink config.json + (2) keyring entry. Wizard does NOT write Pat's config. Without something filling that gap, Pat spawns but has no callsign/MBO config = nonfunctional.

Scope: amend Task 3 (PatProcess in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs) to render Pat's config.json from tuxlink's config + the keyring callsign convention, at Pat-spawn time. Likely involves: read tuxlink config; build Pat config struct/json; write to Pat's expected path (~/.config/pat/config.json); spawn Pat.

Full build-robust-features pipeline per memory feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev (no upstream skip). Pipeline: brainstorm (small; design is settled by cred-handling spec + AMD-13 + this gap) → spec → 5-round adrev → spec revision → plan-write → 4-round plan-review → revision → TDD impl.

Blocks: tuxlink-ln3 impl plan (wizard cluster) — without this amendment landing, the wizard impl would ship but Pat couldn't operate. Wizard impl plan should document this as a HARD prerequisite if not yet done.

title: ADR 0011: Fork Pat for tuxlink (cred-handling + ongoing refactor)
description:
Architectural decision triggered 2026-05-18 during Task 6 brainstorm. Cameron rejected continuing to depend on upstream Pat as-is because Pat persists WL2K passwords to its config.json in plaintext, which is a non-negotiable defect for a production system. Bandaging the symptom at each tuxlink call site (env-var workarounds, temp-file mitigations) is bandaid spiraling.

Decision (Cameron, inline): FORK Pat as a tuxlink-owned variant. Refactor cred-handling to use OS keyring as the first patch. Continue refactoring other Pat limitations as discovered, using the full build-robust-features pipeline per refactor. Open to upstream contributions where the fork's changes are accepted upstream.

This ADR captures: context (the bandaid pattern), decision (fork), alternatives considered (continue-as-is / Rust rewrite / hybrid), consequences (Go codebase to maintain, fork-sync discipline, v0.0.1 scope expansion to include fork-setup + cred refactor + plan amendments for affected tasks 5/6/9), and the transition plan.

Light pipeline: decision is made; ADR is a write-up + Codex round + PR. Full build-robust-features pipeline NOT required for the ADR itself (it's documentation of a made decision). Subsequent tasks (fork-setup, cred-refactor, plan amendments) DO use the full pipeline.

title: Bug: get_tuxlink_sessions.py disagrees with block-main-checkout-race.sh on live-session detection
description:
Per towhee-wren-aspen's 2026-05-18 reviewer response (dev/incidents/2026-05-18-main-checkout-race-hook-loop-reviewer-response.md, reviewer Action 3) — the script and the hook read the same .git/session-leases/*.json files with the same 30-min default cutoff, but in the 2026-05-18 incident the script reported 'No live tuxlink sessions in this repo' while the hook was treating a 4-minutes-ago lease as live. They should agree by construction; they don't. This is a port-quality bug independent of the CLAUDE.md framing fix in PR #39 — even after the framing fix, a script that reports 'no live sessions' while the hook denies will continue to confuse future agents who consult it for legitimate informational purposes (e.g., 'is anyone else working in this repo right now?').

Reviewer's likely causes, in priority order:

1. **Timestamp parse failure (most likely).** The hook writes lastSeenUtc using bash's date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ (6-digit microseconds + trailing Z). Python's datetime.fromisoformat is stricter than people expect about fractional-second precision and trailing-Z handling. The script's parse_iso_utc returns None on parse failure, and on None the lease is silently skipped — causing under-reporting without any error message.

   Reproduce:
       ts=$(jq -r .lastSeenUtc .git/session-leases/<any-session-id>.json)
       python3 -c "
       import sys
       sys.path.insert(0, '.claude/scripts')
       from get_tuxlink_sessions import parse_iso_utc
       print(repr(parse_iso_utc('$ts')))
       "

   If None: that's the bug. Fix: align format on both sides (widen parse_iso_utc OR constrain the hook).

2. **Lease dir path mismatch.** The hook resolves the lease dir via git rev-parse --git-common-dir (= .git/session-leases/). The script resolves via Path(__file__).resolve().parent / '..' / '..' / '.claude' / 'session-leases'. If these resolve to different paths, the script reads an empty/stale dir and reports 'no sessions.'

   Reproduce: print both dirs from a test harness; compare.

3. **File-extension/glob mismatch.** Less likely. The script's sorted(lease_dir.glob('*.json')) should pick up every *.json file. If the hook is writing to a dir without .json extension, the script silently skips.

Priority: P1 (blocks future port-quality regressions of same shape; cheap fix; safety-stack relevance). Should land as its own atomic PR after diagnosis confirms which cause applies. No dependencies on PRs #37/#38/#39/#40.
notes: PR #44 opened 2026-05-18 by oak-fjord-swallow. Tasks 1-7 + 9-10 complete; Task 8 (PARITY-1 pitfalls entry) deferred until PR #43 (superseded PR #40 after rebase) merges and brings HOOK-1+LEASE-1 into feat/v0.0.1. Closes via deliverable on merge. title: CI build-linux path filter excludes frontend (src/**) — frontend-only PRs run NO CI
description: release.yml build-linux pull_request paths = [src-tauri/**, external/tuxlink-pat, .gitmodules, .github/workflows/release.yml]. Frontend-only changes (src/**, index.html, package.json) trigger NO CI at all — discovered when PR #85 (CSS+HTML) ran zero checks (merged on local gates + operator smoke). A frontend change that breaks tsc/vitest/the vite build would NOT be caught by CI. Add a frontend CI job (tsc --noEmit + vitest run, and optionally the vite build) triggered on src/**, index.html, package.json, vite/ts config. Connects to the tuxlink-cbz theme (no automated frontend/visual gate). Found 2026-05-20 (grouse-esker-juniper).

title: Wizard keyring UX: locked login keyring on auto-login Linux triggers a confusing gnome-keyring prompt (not in-app guidance)
description: Found during M1 smoke 2026-05-20 on the dev Pi (auto-login enabled = Raspberry Pi OS default; login Secret Service collection Locked=true). pam_gnome_keyring is configured but cannot unlock the login keyring because auto-login enters no password. The wizard's first keyring write (wizard_persist_cms) then triggers gnome-keyring's OWN unlock dialog, which asks for the keyring password — operators confuse this with the sudo/login password and get rejected. The Rust side is correct (map_keyring_error -> WizardError::Locked; snapshot read before destructive set_password). Gap is operator-experience: (1) the wizard should pre-flight the Secret Service lock state and surface clear in-app guidance + a recovery path (unlock, or set the login keyring password-less for auto-unlock) instead of letting the raw gnome prompt confuse the operator; (2) docs/install.md should document the auto-login keyring caveat for Pi deployments. Bites the common emcomm deployment (auto-login Pi). Not an M1 code blocker; the keyring write itself works once the collection is unlocked.

title: Wizard test-send: Busy from a non-test-send mutex holder leaves window stuck in sending
description: Codex Part-97 re-round of b45f592 (P1 liveness, NOT transmission-safety — no double-TX path). The single-flight tokio::Mutex is shared by wizard_persist_cms, wizard_persist_offline, wizard_run_test_send (wizard.rs). The frontend treats ANY WizardError::Busy as a strict no-op that stays in 'sending'. If the contention came from a persist command or another window (not a concurrent test-send), no TEST_SEND_RESULT ever arrives and the window stays in 'sending' indefinitely. Mitigations: unreachable in the single-window first-run flow (persist completes before test-send), and recoverable via the always-present Skip control during sending. Fix options: track mutex holder/source so Busy-from-test-send vs Busy-from-other is distinguishable, OR add a 'sending' watchdog timeout -> failed. Deferred from M1 by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).

title: Fix File → Quit native handling (post-PR-70 smoke gap)
description:
Smoke test of PR #70 (Task 7 menu bar) on 2026-05-19 surfaced one failure: clicking File → Quit (or pressing Ctrl+Q) does nothing. Root cause: wire_menu_events in src-tauri/src/menu.rs emits the menu:file:quit event to the 'menu' Tauri channel and stops. The React frontend (src/App.tsx — still Tauri-React boilerplate) has no listen('menu', ...) handler, so the event fires into dead air.

Every menu item has the same shape, but Quit is the one item users have a hard expectation about ('Quit must quit').

Fix: special-case Quit in wire_menu_events so it works natively without waiting for Tasks 9+ to wire the frontend listener. ~4 lines:

  if id == 'menu:file:quit' { app_for_handler.exit(0); }

The event still emits, so future frontend listeners (e.g., 'discard unsaved draft?' dialog in Task 14 Compose) can still observe + intercept later.

Discipline: plumbing-class per feedback_discipline_triage_rule — spec is this issue body, TDD-direct, no Codex round.
acceptance_criteria: File → Quit closes the app cleanly. Ctrl+Q closes the app cleanly. menu_test still passes (event manifest unchanged). cargo build --lib still warning-free. Operator-smoke-confirmed before merge.

title: Compose-window capability over-privilege: allow-close/allow-destroy are window-class (can target other windows)
description: Codex P3 (integration-fix re-round, b6331de). compose.json grants core:window:allow-close + allow-destroy. Tauri's window JS sends a caller-supplied label for close/destroy, resolved server-side via manager().get_window(label), so these window-class permissions are NOT scoped to 'self' — a compromised/XSS'd compose window could close/destroy OTHER windows (e.g. main). Normal operation is safe (compose only calls getCurrentWindow().close(); the main-only Rust guard on compose_window_open prevents recursive spawn). allow-destroy is genuinely needed (onCloseRequested uses it when default not prevented), so it can't simply be removed. Bounded surface: close/destroy only — no data exfil or transmission. Fix options: (a) route window-close through a custom Rust command that closes ONLY the calling window and drop the window-class grants from the compose capability; (b) accept the bounded surface as a documented v0.0.1 risk. Hardening; requires an XSS to exploit. Deferred from the integration commit by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).

title: Session-log history ring buffer for session_log_snapshot replay (Task 15 follow-up)
description: Surfaced during integration-commit fix b6331de. session_log_snapshot is now implemented + registered + projects LogLineDto correctly, but returns an EMPTY Vec because no history exists to snapshot: the WinlinkBackend trait exposes only stream_log() (live BoxStream, no replay) and PatBackend uses a tokio::broadcast channel with no snapshot-readable history. Result: a late-mounting SessionLog seeds with [] and only sees live tail from mount onward; spec ~§198 wants the snapshot to seed prior session history. Fix: add a bounded ring buffer to the log mechanism (PatBackend / AppBackend) that session_log_snapshot reads. The command + registration + DTO projection are already in place (b6331de); only the buffer is missing. Only matters once a live session produces history (post tuxlink-22l).

title: Harden compose_window_open: validate/encode draft_id before using in window label + route
description: Codex P2 (integration-commit round, 266353a). compose_window_open uses draft_id directly in both the window label (compose-<draftId>) and the route without encoding/validation (compose_window.rs:51). The frontend routing.ts parser handles encoded/missing/trailing-slash/malformed paths safely, and the normal menu path uses safe generated IDs (newDraftId), so this is NOT the normal path — but an IPC caller passing '/', '?', '#', or malformed '%' could create a label/URL that doesn't mount the intended draft. Fix: validate draft_id (charset/length) and/or percent-encode before label+route construction; reject invalid. v0.0.1-safe (generated IDs only), so hardening. Deferred from the integration commit by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).

title: Wizard MOCKED banner: absent-while-mocked if is_mocked query errors or races a fast mock return
description: Codex Part-97 re-round of b45f592 (P2 visibility, safe-direction failure — no transmission risk). The frontend fails the wizard_test_send_is_mocked query to false (Step3TestSend.tsx:92), so a query error makes the MOCKED banner ABSENT while actually mocked; a fast mocked send can also return before the query resolves. Spec §3.8 wants the banner visible during 'sending' in mock mode so the operator can confirm zero live transmission. Normal local operation shows the banner fine (M1 smoke unaffected). Fix: treat query error as assume-mocked-in-dev / render a pending state, or drive the banner from the same signal the mocked outcome carries. Deferred from M1 by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).

title: Task 11 live-path failure serialization: WizardError::Other{detail:json} should be structured TestSendOutcome::Failed
description: Codex P2 from Task 11 review (carried, not fixed in b45f592). Live test-send failures (and the timeout path) are serialized as WizardError::Other{detail: <JSON string>} in run_test_send_impl / live_path_outcome_to_wizard_error; the frontend does not parse that JSON and renders 'Unexpected error: {json}'. The failed substate still appears, but operator copy is degraded — should be a structured TestSendOutcome::Failed mapped to the spec's failure copy. Live-path only (not exercised in MOCK mode or M1 smoke). Deferred by orchestrator (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20) to keep the consent-state P0 fix tight.

title: Reading pane: enforce read-side byte cap before buffering full HTTP response (Task 13 Codex finding 3)
description: Codex post-subagent review of Task 13 (bd-tuxlink-y5c) found the 2 MiB cap is enforced inside parse_raw_rfc5322 — AFTER message_read awaits backend.read_message_in and PatClient::read buffers the entire HTTP response via resp.bytes().await (pat_client.rs:166). The UI cannot bypass the parser cap, but a buggy/oversized Pat endpoint can force unbounded memory/network work before the cap trips. Pat is trusted localhost so risk is low; this is v0.1 upstream-cap hardening (stream + early-abort on Content-Length / chunk count). Only exercised once live Pat bootstrap (tuxlink-22l) flows real messages. Deferred from the y5c merge per orchestrator triage (grouse-esker-juniper, 2026-05-20).

title: cred-handling spec: use_native_store(true) is stale for keyring 3.6.3
description: Task 10 keyring fix (PR #75, commit 5f269d9) found keyring 3.6.3 has NO use_native_store() fn — backend selection is compile-time via Cargo features (default-features=false + ['sync-secret-service','crypto-rust']). The cred-handling spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-cred-handling-design.md (line ~80 architecture diagram + line ~209 note) still prescribes keyring::use_native_store(true)?, stale keyring-core terminology. Also: the real Secret Service attribute key is 'username' (not 'account') for the Rust keyring crate, matching Pat zalando go-keyring's read — worth documenting the verified cross-process attribute contract. Doc-only fix.

title: Tauri webview shows TV-static / GPU artifacts on first launch (Pi 5, Wayland, webkit2gtk-4.1)
description:
Symptom: every time `pnpm tauri dev` (and presumably `pnpm tauri build` AppImage) launches the Tauri window on the dev Pi 5, the window appears as TV-static / uninitialized-GPU-memory graphical artifacts. Any click or window manipulation triggers a repaint and the page content appears normally.

This is an upstream webkit2gtk + Wayland + Mesa V3D (Pi 5 VideoCore VII GPU) class of bug — the WebKit GPU compositor allocates the webview surface before the first frame is painted, so the window shows whatever's in uninitialized GPU memory until an event forces a repaint.

NOT introduced by tuxlink code. Has been present since the Tauri scaffold was added (commit 52d4181, 2026-04-22). Just easier to notice now after Task 7's menu work made the operator stare at the window carefully.

Mitigation env vars to test (least invasive first):
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 — disables WebKit's DMA-BUF rendering path, known fix for several Wayland-render bugs
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 — disables GPU compositing entirely, software fallback
- GDK_BACKEND=x11 — falls back to XWayland; nuclear option

If any mitigation works, bake it into:
- The dev experience (env var in package.json scripts or .env.local equivalent)
- The release build (tauri.conf.json's runtime config or AppImage launcher script)

For Task 17 (AppImage packaging, tuxlink-cs7): the release artifact MUST ship with the working mitigation env var pre-set so end users don't see TV-static on their Pi/Linux desktops.

Acceptance:
- Operator verifies one of the env vars eliminates the TV-static symptom on their Pi 5
- Working env var is documented in development.md
- AppImage build (Task 17) bakes the env var into the launcher

Priority P3 (polish — doesn't block functionality; v0.0.1 ships with or without this fix per operator call).
